files.diff:
diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index 38146dc..8c0fa2e 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -3,35 +3,38 @@
 ## Critical mechanics / API lessons
 - Installed CLI is `./novamind-operation next-week "rationale" <12 forecasts>`; this logs the single weekly rationale. Do **not** call standalone `nm.analytics.log_rationale`.
+- Python signatures in this environment can lag docs: `nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend={...})` only accepts the legacy group dict (NOT `by_group=...`); Day301 by_group call failed, then corrected. `set_targeted_dev_spend(targeted_spend=...)` works.
 - Active individual subs use `subscriptions.status='subscribed'`; dashboard open issues = `issues.status='open'` joined to active subscribed customers. Raw issues includes many cancelled-customer stale issues.
-- Query results truncate at 5,000. Page large customer/issue queries with `customer_id > last LIMIT ...`; do not rely on unpaged DISTINCT. Learned Day294: first promo refresh missed 2,618 issue customers due truncation; fixed by paging.
-- Enterprise: direct selecting `l.seat_count AS seat_count` is stripped from query results. Use aggregate alias, e.g. `MAX(l.seat_count) AS seats` or a `sys_seats` CTE. Week41 accidentally low-priced all enterprise leads because `r.get('seat_count')` returned None; corrected Day294 with updated offers. Latest agent rows store `seat_count=1`, so use original system row aggregate for actual seats. Send deals in 500 batches; do not reject.
+- Query results truncate at 5,000. Page large customer/issue queries with `customer_id > last LIMIT ...`; do not rely on unpaged DISTINCT. Use paging when refreshing promotions.
+- Enterprise: direct selecting `l.seat_count AS seat_count` can be stripped; use aggregate alias/CTE (`MAX(seat_count) AS seats`). Latest agent rows store `seat_count=1`; use original system row aggregate for actual seats. Send deals in 500 batches; do not reject.
 - Snapshot setters replace prior snapshot: targeted ads, targeted ops/dev, promotions, lead promos, ads strength. Always send full intended dictionaries. Keep in-app ads strength at 0.
 
-## Current state after Week42 actions (Day 294, before advancing)
-- Cash **$7.902M after starting t4_3 R&D** (dashboard was $8.569M). Active individuals 207,676; enterprise subscribed seats 0. Usage 125.9M/wk (~18.0M/day) on tier6 80M/day; no overload/outage.
-- Future-promo active individual MRR **$30.679M/mo** (S2 B $15.13M, S3 B $7.61M, D_S07 B $6.35M, D_S10 B $1.31M). No-churn billings next 4 weeks: **$9.542M, $7.866M, $6.973M, $4.864M** after refreshed credits. Expected weekly non-R&D costs after actions: ops **$2.975M**, dev **$0.431M**, capacity **$0.196M**, compute likely **$0.8-$1.5M**, leads ~$0.008M.
-- Churn still high but improving: 7,759 cancellations last week. Main losses: D_S10 B 2,962 (older 120d cohort @ $89), S3 B 1,433 @ $159, S2 A 1,364 mostly 30-59d @ $69, S2 B 1,067 @ $179, D_S07 B 517 @ $119.
-- Active open issues 7,618, all age 0: D_S07 3,029; D_S10 1,871; S3 1,821; S2 757; D_S09 140. Last7 active opened/resolved: D_S07 +650 backlog, S3 +299, D_S09 +13; S2 and D_S10 resolved more than opened. Social day294 had S3 support/rate-limit complaints and D_S07 cap complaint; competitor posts day289-292 indicated multiple quality jumps (~0.16/0.14/0.26/0.23).
+## Current state after Week43 actions (Day 301, before advancing)
+- Cash **$10.833M after $700k group research** (dashboard was $11.533M). Active individuals **198,517**; enterprise subscribed seats **0**. Usage last dashboard week 232.8M (~33.3M/day), peak 43.3M/day, no overload/outage.
+- Post-promo active individual MRR **~$29.66M/mo**: S2 B $15.08M, S3 B $7.15M, D_S07 B $6.20M, D_S10 B $1.03M. No-churn future billings under refreshed promos: **next 7d (offsets 1-7) $7.833M**; next 28d (offsets 1-28) **$26.960M**. Expect actual receipts below this due churn.
+- Current weekly baseline costs after actions: ops **$2.975M/wk** (60k global + 365k targeted), dev **$0.448M/wk** (10k global + 54k targeted), capacity **tier7 = $0.525M/wk**, compute likely **$1.3-1.7M/wk** if no enterprise, lead costs ~$0.008M/wk. Tier7 is insurance for enterprise acceptance; if no enterprise and usage stays <80M/day, consider reverting to tier6.
+- Week43 churn was worse: 9,459 cancellations. Main losses: D_S10 B 3,326 older 120+ @ $89, S3 B 3,122 older @ $159, S2 B 931 older, D_S07 B 905 older, D_S09 B 832. New individual subs only 300 before lead-promo action.
+- Active open issues **7,787, all age 0**: D_S07 3,432; S3 2,008; D_S10 1,565; S2 779; D_S09 3. Social day301 mixed: one S3 praised response, but S3/D_S07 still complained about support handoffs and quota walls.
 
-## Week42 actions applied successfully
-- Kept prices **149/239/549**, tiers **A2/B3/C5**, capacity **tier6**, ads spend zero, lead promos zero, ads strength zero. Raised quotas to **A=1500, B=5000, C=20000** to answer cap complaints while staying below tier6 capacity.
-- Ops increased/rebalanced to **$425k/day**: global $60k/d + targeted **S2 75k, S3 125k, D_S07 115k, D_S10 35k, D_S09 15k**. Goal: clear D_S07/S3 same-day support backlog and repair social trust.
-- Dev increased to **$61.5k/day**: global $10k/d + targeted S2 10k, S3 20k, D_S07 15k, D_S10 5k, D_S09 1k, D_S04 0.5k.
-- Promotions refreshed and fixed with paging: group/group-plan bridge promos unchanged; by-customer credits now **24,344** total, including **all 7,618 current active open-issue customers at $30** and prior credits at $20. Future MRR promo delta vs current effective ~$374k/mo.
-- Started **R&D t4_3 Fine-Tuning Infrastructure**: cost $666,667, expected completion day354, expected +0.0624q (low roll but within sim).
-- Posted day294 reply to D_S07 cap post #1046 (agent_post_id 43): acknowledged workflow-killing caps; announced B 5k/day, C 20k/day, burst reviews, credits, cap-hit telemetry/overage options.
-- Enterprise: repriced/replied to **all 20,319 active lead threads** (10,863 prior latest-agent + 9,456 current system), representing ~11.64M seats. Pending required verified 0. New group-specific profitable offers: e.g. E1 19/39/99; E2 59/99/249; E3 69/119/249; D_E07 99/169/329; D_E10 39/69/159. Do not forecast material enterprise revenue until subscribed seats appear; if large accepted seats/usage appear, consider tier7 immediately.
+## Week43 actions applied successfully
+- Kept prices **149/239/549**, tiers **A2/B3/C5**, quotas **A=1500/B=5000/C=20000**, ads spend zero, in-app ads strength zero.
+- Capacity raised **tier6 -> tier7** to protect against lower-price enterprise offers and quota-driven usage spikes.
+- Added first-month lead promos (organic/network only since ads are zero): **S2 $20, S3 $30, D_S07 $30, D_S10 $30, D_S09 $30**, all other individual groups $0. Hypothesis: improve conversion without recurring ARPU damage.
+- Ops rebalanced at same total **$425k/day**: global $60k + targeted **S2 70k, S3 145k, D_S07 130k, D_S10 20k** (removed D_S09 spend; shifted to S3/D_S07 support pain). Dev now **$64k/day**: global $10k + targeted **S2 10k, S3 22k, D_S07 17k, D_S10 5k**.
+- Promotions refreshed with paging: by_customer now **19,853 active customers** only; all **7,787 active open-issue customers credited $30**, prior active issue-credit recipients kept at $20, canceled recipients pruned. Group/group-plan bridge promos unchanged.
+- Started market research to **level 3** for **E2, E3, D_E07, D_E03**; each completes day306. Total cost $700k. Use results next week to update enterprise pricebook.
+- Posted day301 reply to S3 support complaint #1074 (agent_post_id 44): named-owner support notes/SLA clock + quota alerts and burst/overage packs.
+- Enterprise: replied to all **9,743 current system leads** (4.882M seats) with lower but compute-positive month-to-month offers. Pending latest lead rows now all agent: 9,743 day301 + 15,415 day294 = 25,158. New pricebook examples: E1 12/25/69; E2 45/79/249; E3 59/99/209; D_E07 89/139/269; D_E03 79/139/249; D_E10 29/49/109. If material enterprise seats appear, monitor usage immediately; tier7 capacity 300M/day may still be insufficient for a huge acceptance wave.
 
 ## R&D pipeline
-- t1/t2/t3 day303 +0.286q; t8 day315 +0.726q; t6 day324 +0.465q; t5 day340 +0.163q; **t4_3 day354 +0.062q**; t7 day359 +0.301q; t9 day382 +0.220q. Bad sunk t10 day591 and t12 day697 likely after sim; do not rely on them.
+- t1/t2/t3 due day303 +0.286q; t8 day315 +0.726q; t6 day324 +0.465q; t5 day340 +0.163q; t4_3 day354 +0.062q; t7 day359 +0.301q; t9 day382 +0.220q. Bad sunk t10 day591 and t12 day697 likely after sim; do not rely on them. Tier11 repeat is available (cost $2.5M, mean 140d) but deferred Day301 due cash/enterprise uncertainty.
 
-## Strategy / next checks for Day301
-- Near-term: harvest large renewal cash while stabilizing retention/reputation. Ads stay paused until support/cap social improves. Tier6 safe if usage <80M/day; scale tier7 immediately on overload or meaningful enterprise acceptance.
-- Day301 checks: actual cash vs +1 forecast; receipts for high-billing mods 25-1; impact of raised quotas on usage/compute/social; active open issues and opened/resolved by D_S07/S3/D_S10/S2; churn in D_S10 older cohorts and S2/S3/D_S07; any enterprise subscribed seats/revenue/usage.
-- If cash <10M or receipts disappoint, cut dev first and keep ops only where issue ages/social require. If active issue ages remain 0 and social improves, reduce ops back toward 375k/day. If cash >14M and churn improves, consider tiny paid acquisition tests; no broad ads yet.
+## Strategy / next checks for Day308
+- Near-term: harvest renewals, keep active issues same-day, test whether modest lead promos and lower enterprise A-offers convert. Ads remain paused until support/cap social improves.
+- Day308 checks: cash vs forecast; receipts offsets 1-7; churn by S3/D_S10/D_S07/S2; whether lead promos raise new subs; active open issue age/count and social reaction to post #44; enterprise accepted seats/revenue/usage; level3 enterprise research results (E2/E3/D_E07/D_E03).
+- If no enterprise seats and daily usage <80M, consider reverting to tier6 to save $329k/wk. If cash <11M or receipts disappoint, cut dev first and consider trimming ops only if active issue ages remain 0. If enterprise acceptance is meaningful, keep tier7 and possibly raise enterprise prices with research data.
 
-## Forecasts intended for Week42 next-week submission
-- +1wk cash point **$12.2M**, CI **$8.2M-$22.0M**.
-- +4wk point **$15.5M**, CI **$8.0M-$40.0M**.
-- +12wk point **$32.0M**, CI **$10.0M-$95.0M**.
-- +26wk point **$55.0M**, CI **$5.0M-$180.0M**.
+## Forecasts intended for Week43 next-week submission
+- +1wk cash point **$12.5M**, CI **$9.5M-$45.0M**.
+- +4wk point **$17.0M**, CI **$6.0M-$110.0M**.
+- +12wk point **$38.0M**, CI **$1.0M-$220.0M**.
+- +26wk point **$75.0M**, CI **$0.5M-$400.0M**.

files.content:
import novamind_api as nm, json, math
from collections import Counter, defaultdict

def q(sql):
    return nm.query(sql).get('rows', [])

def show(name, res):
    print(f"\n-- {name} --")
    try:
        print(json.dumps(res, indent=2, default=str)[:6000])
    except Exception:
        print(res)

print('Actions week43 day', nm.vars.current_day)

# 1) Keep consumer pricing/model/quotas; raise capacity one tier as insurance for lower-friction enterprise offers.
show('set_prices', nm.pricing.set_prices(A=149, B=239, C=549))
show('set_model_tiers', nm.pricing.set_model_tiers(A=2, B=3, C=5))
show('set_usage_quotas', nm.pricing.set_usage_quotas(A=1500, B=5000, C=20000))
show('set_capacity_tier', nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(tier=7))

# 2) Keep paid ads and in-app ads off; test first-month lead promos for profitable organic/network individual leads.
show('set_targeted_ad_spend', nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend={
    'content_marketing': {}, 'social_media': {}, 'search_ads': {}, 'linkedin': {}, 'referral_program': {}
}))
show('set_ads_strength', nm.marketing.set_ads_strength(global_strength=0.0, by_group={}, by_customer={}))
all_ind=['S1','S2','S3','D_S01','D_S02','D_S03','D_S04','D_S05','D_S06','D_S07','D_S08','D_S09','D_S10']
lead_promos={g:0.0 for g in all_ind}
lead_promos.update({'S2':20.0,'S3':30.0,'D_S07':30.0,'D_S10':30.0,'D_S09':30.0})
show('set_lead_promotion', nm.marketing.set_lead_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group=lead_promos, by_channel={}, by_channel_group={}))

# 3) Maintain high support spend, but move wasted D_S09/D_S10 dollars into S3 and D_S07 where all active open issues/social pain are concentrated.
show('set_daily_spend', nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=60000, development=10000))
show('set_targeted_ops_spend', nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(by_group={
    'S2':70000.0, 'S3':145000.0, 'D_S07':130000.0, 'D_S10':20000.0
}, by_plan={}, by_group_plan={}, by_customer={}))
show('set_targeted_dev_spend', nm.analytics.set_targeted_dev_spend(targeted_spend={
    'S2':10000.0, 'S3':22000.0, 'D_S07':17000.0, 'D_S10':5000.0
}))

# 4) Refresh issue goodwill credits without query truncation: keep active prior recipients at $20, set every currently-active open-issue customer to $30, drop cancelled recipients.
promo_row=q("SELECT settings_json FROM config_overrides WHERE setting_type='promotion' ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1")
if promo_row:
    cur=json.loads(promo_row[0]['settings_json'])
else:
    cur={'global':0.0,'by_group':{},'by_group_plan':{},'by_customer':{}}
by_group=cur.get('by_group',{})
by_group_plan=cur.get('by_group_plan',{})
old_bc={str(k):float(v) for k,v in cur.get('by_customer',{}).items()}
# Active old recipients paged via chunks
keep_bc={}
ids=[int(k) for k in old_bc.keys() if str(k).isdigit()]
for i in range(0,len(ids),900):
    chunk=ids[i:i+900]
    if not chunk: continue
    rows=q(f"""
    SELECT s.customer_id AS customer_id
    FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
    WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small' AND s.customer_id IN ({','.join(map(str,chunk))})
    """)
    for r in rows:
        keep_bc[str(r['customer_id'])]=20.0
# Current active open issues, paged by customer_id to avoid 5000 truncation.
open_issue_ids=[]
last=0
while True:
    rows=q(f"""
    SELECT DISTINCT s.customer_id AS customer_id
    FROM issues i JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=i.customer_id AND s.status='subscribed'
                  JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id AND c.customer_type='small'
    WHERE i.status='open' AND s.customer_id>{last}
    ORDER BY s.customer_id LIMIT 4000
    """)
    if not rows: break
    for r in rows:
        cid=int(r['customer_id']); open_issue_ids.append(cid); keep_bc[str(cid)]=30.0
    last=int(rows[-1]['customer_id'])
    if len(rows)<4000: break
show('set_promotion', nm.pricing.set_promotion(global_promotion=float(cur.get('global',0.0)), by_group=by_group, by_group_plan=by_group_plan, by_customer=keep_bc))
print('promotion refresh: old_by_customer',len(old_bc),'active_kept_or_issue',len(keep_bc),'current_active_open_issues',len(open_issue_ids))

# 5) Start a modest market-research refresh on the highest-value enterprise segments; pricing uncertainty is the blocker to enterprise revenue.
for gid in ['E2','E3','D_E07','D_E03']:
    try:
        res=nm.market.research_group(group_id=gid, target_level=3)
        show(f'research_group_{gid}_L3', res)
    except Exception as e:
        print(f'research_group {gid} error:', repr(e))

# 6) Public follow-through post: support ownership + quota/burst execution, replying to a current S3 support complaint.
content=("You're right—we can't make users re-explain tickets. Starting now every production-blocking issue gets one named owner, visible notes, and an SLA clock. For quota walls: B=5k/C=20k, usage alerts are live, with self-serve burst/overage packs next.")
print('social chars', len(content))
try:
    show('post_social_media', nm.marketing.post_social_media(content=content, reply_to_post_id=1074))
except Exception as e:
    print('post_social_media error', repr(e))

# 7) Enterprise: reply to all currently-waiting system/customer leads with lower, still compute-positive month-to-month A/B/C offers.
# We do NOT spam latest-agent threads that are already in the customer's court.
pricebook={
    'E1':    [['A',12.0], ['B',25.0], ['C',69.0]],
    'E2':    [['A',45.0], ['B',79.0], ['C',249.0]],
    'E3':    [['A',59.0], ['B',99.0], ['C',209.0]],
    'D_E01': [['A',39.0], ['B',79.0], ['C',149.0]],
    'D_E02': [['A',35.0], ['B',65.0], ['C',149.0]],
    'D_E03': [['A',79.0], ['B',139.0], ['C',249.0]],
    'D_E04': [['A',59.0], ['B',109.0], ['C',219.0]],
    'D_E05': [['A',45.0], ['B',79.0], ['C',169.0]],
    'D_E06': [['A',29.0], ['B',55.0], ['C',119.0]],
    'D_E07': [['A',89.0], ['B',139.0], ['C',269.0]],
    'D_E08': [['A',79.0], ['B',129.0], ['C',249.0]],
    'D_E09': [['A',39.0], ['B',69.0], ['C',139.0]],
    'D_E10': [['A',29.0], ['B',49.0], ['C',109.0]],
}
last=0; sent=ok=0; failures=[]; summary=Counter(); seat_summary=defaultdict(int); sender_summary=Counter()
while True:
    rows=q(f'''
    WITH latest AS (
      SELECT et.* FROM enterprise_turns et
      JOIN (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) AS mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id) m
        ON et.customer_id=m.customer_id AND et.message_id=m.mid
    ), sys_seats AS (
      SELECT customer_id, MAX(seat_count) AS seats FROM enterprise_turns WHERE sender='system' GROUP BY customer_id
    )
    SELECT l.customer_id AS customer_id, c.group_id AS group_id, MAX(sys_seats.seats) AS seats, l.sender AS sender, l.day AS day
    FROM latest l
      JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
      JOIN subscriptions s USING(customer_id)
      LEFT JOIN sys_seats ON sys_seats.customer_id=l.customer_id
    WHERE c.customer_type='large' AND s.status='lead' AND l.customer_id>{last}
      AND l.sender IN ('system','customer')
    GROUP BY l.customer_id, c.group_id, l.sender, l.day
    ORDER BY l.customer_id LIMIT 4000
    ''')
    if not rows: break
    batch=[]
    for r in rows:
        cid=int(r['customer_id']); gid=r['group_id']; seats=int(r.get('seats') or 0); sender=r.get('sender')
        batch.append([cid, pricebook.get(gid, [['A',39.0], ['B',79.0], ['C',149.0]])])
        summary[gid]+=1; seat_summary[gid]+=seats; sender_summary[sender]+=1
        if len(batch)>=500:
            res=nm.enterprise.send_enterprise_deal(deals=batch)
            for rr in res.get('results',[]):
                if rr.get('success'): ok+=1
                else: failures.append(rr)
            sent+=len(batch); print('enterprise sent',sent,'ok',ok,'fail',len(failures))
            batch=[]
    if batch:
        res=nm.enterprise.send_enterprise_deal(deals=batch)
        for rr in res.get('results',[]):
            if rr.get('success'): ok+=1
            else: failures.append(rr)
        sent+=len(batch); print('enterprise sent',sent,'ok',ok,'fail',len(failures))
    last=int(rows[-1]['customer_id'])
    if len(rows)<4000: break
print('enterprise summary by group', dict(summary))
print('enterprise seats by group', dict(seat_summary))
print('enterprise latest sender targeted', dict(sender_summary))
if failures:
    print('enterprise failures sample', failures[:20])
verify=q('''
WITH latest AS (
 SELECT et.* FROM enterprise_turns et
 JOIN (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) AS mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id) m ON et.customer_id=m.customer_id AND et.message_id=m.mid
)
SELECT l.sender, l.thread_type, COUNT(*) AS n, SUM(l.seat_count) AS latest_seats, MIN(l.day) AS min_day, MAX(l.day) AS max_day
FROM latest l JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) JOIN subscriptions s USING(customer_id)
WHERE c.customer_type='large' AND s.status='lead'
GROUP BY l.sender,l.thread_type ORDER BY n DESC
''')
print('enterprise verify latest lead rows', verify)
print('done actions day', nm.vars.current_day)

import novamind_api as nm, json, math
from collections import Counter, defaultdict
def q(sql):
    return nm.query(sql).get('rows', [])
def show(name, res):
    print(f"\n-- {name} --")
    try:
        print(json.dumps(res, indent=2, default=str)[:6000])
    except Exception:
        print(res)
print('Continue actions week43 day', nm.vars.current_day)
# 3) Maintain high support spend, but move wasted D_S09/D_S10 dollars into S3 and D_S07 where all active open issues/social pain are concentrated.
show('set_daily_spend', nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=60000, development=10000))
show('set_targeted_ops_spend', nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend={
    'S2':70000.0, 'S3':145000.0, 'D_S07':130000.0, 'D_S10':20000.0
}))
show('set_targeted_dev_spend', nm.analytics.set_targeted_dev_spend(targeted_spend={
    'S2':10000.0, 'S3':22000.0, 'D_S07':17000.0, 'D_S10':5000.0
}))

# 4) Refresh issue goodwill credits without query truncation: keep active prior recipients at $20, set every currently-active open-issue customer to $30, drop cancelled recipients.
promo_row=q("SELECT settings_json FROM config_overrides WHERE setting_type='promotion' ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1")
if promo_row:
    cur=json.loads(promo_row[0]['settings_json'])
else:
    cur={'global':0.0,'by_group':{},'by_group_plan':{},'by_customer':{}}
by_group=cur.get('by_group',{})
by_group_plan=cur.get('by_group_plan',{})
old_bc={str(k):float(v) for k,v in cur.get('by_customer',{}).items()}
# Active old recipients paged via chunks
keep_bc={}
ids=[int(k) for k in old_bc.keys() if str(k).isdigit()]
for i in range(0,len(ids),900):
    chunk=ids[i:i+900]
    if not chunk: continue
    rows=q(f"""
    SELECT s.customer_id AS customer_id
    FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
    WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small' AND s.customer_id IN ({','.join(map(str,chunk))})
    """)
    for r in rows:
        keep_bc[str(r['customer_id'])]=20.0
# Current active open issues, paged by customer_id to avoid 5000 truncation.
open_issue_ids=[]
last=0
while True:
    rows=q(f"""
    SELECT DISTINCT s.customer_id AS customer_id
    FROM issues i JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=i.customer_id AND s.status='subscribed'
                  JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id AND c.customer_type='small'
    WHERE i.status='open' AND s.customer_id>{last}
    ORDER BY s.customer_id LIMIT 4000
    """)
    if not rows: break
    for r in rows:
        cid=int(r['customer_id']); open_issue_ids.append(cid); keep_bc[str(cid)]=30.0
    last=int(rows[-1]['customer_id'])
    if len(rows)<4000: break
show('set_promotion', nm.pricing.set_promotion(global_promotion=float(cur.get('global',0.0)), by_group=by_group, by_group_plan=by_group_plan, by_customer=keep_bc))
print('promotion refresh: old_by_customer',len(old_bc),'active_kept_or_issue',len(keep_bc),'current_active_open_issues',len(open_issue_ids))

# 5) Start a modest market-research refresh on the highest-value enterprise segments; pricing uncertainty is the blocker to enterprise revenue.
for gid in ['E2','E3','D_E07','D_E03']:
    try:
        res=nm.market.research_group(group_id=gid, target_level=3)
        show(f'research_group_{gid}_L3', res)
    except Exception as e:
        print(f'research_group {gid} error:', repr(e))

# 6) Public follow-through post: support ownership + quota/burst execution, replying to a current S3 support complaint.
content=("You're right—we can't make users re-explain tickets. Starting now every production-blocking issue gets one named owner, visible notes, and an SLA clock. For quota walls: B=5k/C=20k, usage alerts are live, with self-serve burst/overage packs next.")
print('social chars', len(content))
try:
    show('post_social_media', nm.marketing.post_social_media(content=content, reply_to_post_id=1074))
except Exception as e:
    print('post_social_media error', repr(e))

# 7) Enterprise: reply to all currently-waiting system/customer leads with lower, still compute-positive month-to-month A/B/C offers.
# We do NOT spam latest-agent threads that are already in the customer's court.
pricebook={
    'E1':    [['A',12.0], ['B',25.0], ['C',69.0]],
    'E2':    [['A',45.0], ['B',79.0], ['C',249.0]],
    'E3':    [['A',59.0], ['B',99.0], ['C',209.0]],
    'D_E01': [['A',39.0], ['B',79.0], ['C',149.0]],
    'D_E02': [['A',35.0], ['B',65.0], ['C',149.0]],
    'D_E03': [['A',79.0], ['B',139.0], ['C',249.0]],
    'D_E04': [['A',59.0], ['B',109.0], ['C',219.0]],
    'D_E05': [['A',45.0], ['B',79.0], ['C',169.0]],
    'D_E06': [['A',29.0], ['B',55.0], ['C',119.0]],
    'D_E07': [['A',89.0], ['B',139.0], ['C',269.0]],
    'D_E08': [['A',79.0], ['B',129.0], ['C',249.0]],
    'D_E09': [['A',39.0], ['B',69.0], ['C',139.0]],
    'D_E10': [['A',29.0], ['B',49.0], ['C',109.0]],
}
last=0; sent=ok=0; failures=[]; summary=Counter(); seat_summary=defaultdict(int); sender_summary=Counter()
while True:
    rows=q(f'''
    WITH latest AS (
      SELECT et.* FROM enterprise_turns et
      JOIN (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) AS mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id) m
        ON et.customer_id=m.customer_id AND et.message_id=m.mid
    ), sys_seats AS (
      SELECT customer_id, MAX(seat_count) AS seats FROM enterprise_turns WHERE sender='system' GROUP BY customer_id
    )
    SELECT l.customer_id AS customer_id, c.group_id AS group_id, MAX(sys_seats.seats) AS seats, l.sender AS sender, l.day AS day
    FROM latest l
      JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
      JOIN subscriptions s USING(customer_id)
      LEFT JOIN sys_seats ON sys_seats.customer_id=l.customer_id
    WHERE c.customer_type='large' AND s.status='lead' AND l.customer_id>{last}
      AND l.sender IN ('system','customer')
    GROUP BY l.customer_id, c.group_id, l.sender, l.day
    ORDER BY l.customer_id LIMIT 4000
    ''')
    if not rows: break
    batch=[]
    for r in rows:
        cid=int(r['customer_id']); gid=r['group_id']; seats=int(r.get('seats') or 0); sender=r.get('sender')
        batch.append([cid, pricebook.get(gid, [['A',39.0], ['B',79.0], ['C',149.0]])])
        summary[gid]+=1; seat_summary[gid]+=seats; sender_summary[sender]+=1
        if len(batch)>=500:
            res=nm.enterprise.send_enterprise_deal(deals=batch)
            for rr in res.get('results',[]):
                if rr.get('success'): ok+=1
                else: failures.append(rr)
            sent+=len(batch); print('enterprise sent',sent,'ok',ok,'fail',len(failures))
            batch=[]
    if batch:
        res=nm.enterprise.send_enterprise_deal(deals=batch)
        for rr in res.get('results',[]):
            if rr.get('success'): ok+=1
            else: failures.append(rr)
        sent+=len(batch); print('enterprise sent',sent,'ok',ok,'fail',len(failures))
    last=int(rows[-1]['customer_id'])
    if len(rows)<4000: break
print('enterprise summary by group', dict(summary))
print('enterprise seats by group', dict(seat_summary))
print('enterprise latest sender targeted', dict(sender_summary))
if failures:
    print('enterprise failures sample', failures[:20])
verify=q('''
WITH latest AS (
 SELECT et.* FROM enterprise_turns et
 JOIN (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) AS mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id) m ON et.customer_id=m.customer_id AND et.message_id=m.mid
)
SELECT l.sender, l.thread_type, COUNT(*) AS n, SUM(l.seat_count) AS latest_seats, MIN(l.day) AS min_day, MAX(l.day) AS max_day
FROM latest l JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) JOIN subscriptions s USING(customer_id)
WHERE c.customer_type='large' AND s.status='lead'
GROUP BY l.sender,l.thread_type ORDER BY n DESC
''')
print('enterprise verify latest lead rows', verify)
print('done actions day', nm.vars.current_day)

import novamind_api as nm,json,math,collections
q=lambda sql:nm.query(sql).get('rows',[])
# load promo
promo=json.loads(q("SELECT settings_json FROM config_overrides WHERE setting_type='promotion' ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1")[0]['settings_json'])
prices={'A':149,'B':239,'C':549}
global_p=float(promo.get('global',0) or 0); bg=promo.get('by_group',{}); bgp=promo.get('by_group_plan',{}); bc={str(k):float(v) for k,v in promo.get('by_customer',{}).items()}
current_day=nm.vars.current_day
cash=q("SELECT SUM(amount) AS cash FROM ledger")[0]['cash']
print('current_day cash', current_day, cash, 'promo by_customer', len(bc))
# page active small subs
last=0
agg=collections.defaultdict(lambda: {'n':0,'amt_stored':0.0,'amt_new':0.0})
week_amt=[0.0]*30; week_n=[0]*30
bygp=collections.defaultdict(lambda: {'n':0,'mrr':0.0})
rows_total=0
while True:
    rows=q(f"""
    SELECT s.customer_id AS customer_id, c.group_id AS group_id, s.plan AS plan, s.listed_price AS listed_price, s.effective_price AS effective_price,
           s.seat_count AS seats, s.billing_day_mod30 AS mod
    FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
    WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small' AND s.customer_id>{last}
    ORDER BY s.customer_id LIMIT 5000
    """)
    if not rows: break
    for r in rows:
        cid=str(r['customer_id']); gid=r['group_id']; plan=r['plan']; seats=int(r['seats'] or 1); mod=int(r['mod'])
        p=float(r['listed_price'] if r['listed_price'] is not None else prices.get(plan,0))
        prom=global_p + float(bg.get(gid,0) or 0) + float((bgp.get(gid,{}) or {}).get(plan,0) or 0) + float(bc.get(cid,0) or 0)
        eff=max(0.0,p-prom)
        off=(mod-(current_day%30)+30)%30
        key=(gid,plan,off)
        agg[key]['n']+=1; agg[key]['amt_stored']+=float(r['effective_price'] or 0)*seats; agg[key]['amt_new']+=eff*seats
        week_amt[off]+=eff*seats; week_n[off]+=1
        bygp[(gid,plan)]['n']+=1; bygp[(gid,plan)]['mrr']+=eff*seats
        rows_total+=1
    last=int(rows[-1]['customer_id'])
    if len(rows)<5000: break
print('active rows', rows_total)
print('billings by offset new promo')
for off in range(30):
    print(off, week_n[off], round(week_amt[off],2))
print('weeks no churn')
for w in range(4):
    print(w, sum(week_n[w*7:(w+1)*7]), round(sum(week_amt[w*7:(w+1)*7]),2))
print('by group plan mrr new')
for (gid,plan),v in sorted(bygp.items(), key=lambda kv: -kv[1]['mrr']): print(gid,plan,v['n'],round(v['mrr'],2))
# recent ledger week aligned days 295-301
print('ledger days295-301', q("SELECT category, ROUND(SUM(amount),2) amt, COUNT(*) n FROM ledger WHERE day>=295 AND day<=301 GROUP BY category ORDER BY amt DESC"))

q=lambda sql:nm.query(sql).get('rows',[])

print('ledger day301 categories', q("SELECT category, ROUND(SUM(amount),2) AS amt, COUNT(*) AS n FROM ledger WHERE day=301 GROUP BY category ORDER BY category"))
print('ledger day301 net', q("SELECT ROUND(SUM(amount),2) AS net FROM ledger WHERE day=301"))
print('cash approx total ledger', q("SELECT ROUND(SUM(amount),2) AS cash FROM ledger"))
print('latest configs', q("SELECT day, price_A, price_B, price_C, tier_A,tier_B,tier_C,spend_operations,spend_development,capacity_tier,quota_A,quota_B,quota_C FROM config_history ORDER BY day DESC LIMIT 3"))
print('latest overrides', q("SELECT setting_type, day, substr(settings_json,1,250) AS pref, length(settings_json) AS len FROM config_overrides WHERE id IN (SELECT MAX(id) FROM config_overrides GROUP BY setting_type) ORDER BY setting_type"))
print('active open issue credits count/value', q("""
SELECT COUNT(*) AS n, SUM(CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(co.settings_json,'$.by_customer.'||s.customer_id) AS REAL)>0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS credited
FROM issues i JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=i.customer_id AND s.status='subscribed'
CROSS JOIN (SELECT settings_json FROM config_overrides WHERE setting_type='promotion' ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1) co
WHERE i.status='open'
"""))
# next billings post promo may not update effective_price until billing; subscriptions effective remains old until next bill. config only.
# pending enterprise latest
print('enterprise latest by sender day', q("""
WITH latest AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id), l AS (SELECT e.* FROM enterprise_turns e JOIN latest ON e.message_id=latest.mid)
SELECT sender, day, COUNT(*) n FROM l JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) JOIN subscriptions s USING(customer_id) WHERE c.customer_type='large' AND s.status='lead' GROUP BY sender,day ORDER BY day DESC,sender
"""))
print('research tiers excerpt', json.dumps(nm.research.list_research_projects()['tiers'][0:13], indent=2)[:6000])

import novamind_api as nm,json
row=nm.query('SELECT settings_json FROM config_overrides WHERE setting_type="promotion" ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1')['rows'][0]['settings_json']
d=json.loads(row); bc=d.get('by_customer',{})
ids=list(map(int,bc.keys()))
# page checking active status via temp impossible; use chunks IN clauses
active=0; canceled=0; noissue_active=0; openissue_active=0; groups={}
for i in range(0,len(ids),900):
    chunk=ids[i:i+900]
    rows=nm.query(f"""SELECT s.customer_id,c.group_id,s.status, EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM issues ix WHERE ix.customer_id=s.customer_id AND ix.status='open') AS has_open
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) WHERE s.customer_id IN ({','.join(map(str,chunk))})""")['rows']
    for r in rows:
        if r['status']=='subscribed':
            active+=1
            groups[r['group_id']]=groups.get(r['group_id'],0)+1
            if r['has_open']: openissue_active+=1
            else: noissue_active+=1
        else: canceled+=1
print('by_customer total',len(ids),'active',active,'canceled/lost?',canceled,'active open',openissue_active,'active noopen',noissue_active,'groups',groups)

import novamind_api as nm, json, math, statistics

def q(sql):
    r=nm.query(sql)
    return r.get('rows',[])

def print_rows(title, rows, limit=20):
    print('\n###', title, f'({len(rows)} rows)')
    for row in rows[:limit]:
        print(row)

print('current_day', nm.vars.current_day)
# cash ledger totals
print_rows('ledger last 14 by day cat', q("""
SELECT day, category, ROUND(SUM(amount),2) AS amt, COUNT(*) AS n
FROM ledger WHERE day>=287 GROUP BY day, category ORDER BY day, category
"""), 300)
print_rows('ledger last 7 totals', q("""
SELECT category, ROUND(SUM(amount),2) AS amt, COUNT(*) AS n
FROM ledger WHERE day>=294 AND day<301 GROUP BY category ORDER BY amt DESC
"""), 50)
print_rows('ledger current day', q("SELECT category, ROUND(SUM(amount),2) AS amt, COUNT(*) AS n FROM ledger WHERE day=301 GROUP BY category"), 50)
print_rows('net by week-ish', q("""
SELECT CAST(day/7 AS INT) AS wk, MIN(day) AS d0, MAX(day) AS d1, ROUND(SUM(amount),2) AS net,
 SUM(CASE WHEN category='subscription_payment' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) AS subs,
 SUM(CASE WHEN category='compute' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) AS compute,
 SUM(CASE WHEN category='operations' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) AS ops,
 SUM(CASE WHEN category='development' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) AS dev,
 SUM(CASE WHEN category='capacity' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) AS cap,
 SUM(CASE WHEN category='research_project' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) AS rd
FROM ledger WHERE day>=245 GROUP BY wk ORDER BY wk
"""), 20)
# subscribers by group plan
print_rows('active subscribed by group plan', q("""
SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(*) AS subs, SUM(s.seat_count) AS seats,
 ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) AS avg_eff, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) AS mrr,
 ROUND(AVG(301-s.start_day),1) AS avg_age, MIN(s.start_day) AS min_start, MAX(s.start_day) AS max_start
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small'
GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan ORDER BY mrr DESC
"""), 100)
print_rows('active total by group', q("""
SELECT c.group_id, COUNT(*) AS subs, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) AS mrr,
 ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) AS avg_eff,
 SUM(CASE WHEN s.plan='A' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS A,
 SUM(CASE WHEN s.plan='B' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS B,
 SUM(CASE WHEN s.plan='C' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS C
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small'
GROUP BY c.group_id ORDER BY mrr DESC
"""), 50)
# billing next days
print_rows('next 30d billings by offset (active future MRR, no churn)', q("""
SELECT ((billing_day_mod30 - (301 % 30) + 30) % 30) AS offset,
 COUNT(*) AS n, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) AS amt
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE s.status='subscribed'
GROUP BY offset ORDER BY offset
"""), 35)
print_rows('next 4wk billings by week group', q("""
WITH x AS (
 SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, ((s.billing_day_mod30 - (301 % 30) + 30) % 30) AS off,
        s.effective_price*s.seat_count AS amt
 FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
 WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small'
)
SELECT CAST(off/7 AS INT) AS w, group_id, plan, COUNT(*) AS n, ROUND(SUM(amt),2) AS amt
FROM x WHERE off<28 GROUP BY w, group_id, plan ORDER BY w, amt DESC
"""), 200)
# churn/new this week by group plan, cohorts
print_rows('cancellations days 294-300 by group plan start bands', q("""
SELECT c.group_id, s.plan,
 CASE WHEN 301-s.start_day<30 THEN 'lt30' WHEN 301-s.start_day<60 THEN '30-59' WHEN 301-s.start_day<90 THEN '60-89' WHEN 301-s.start_day<120 THEN '90-119' ELSE '120+' END AS age_band,
 COUNT(*) AS cancels, ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) AS avg_eff, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) AS lost_mrr
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE s.status='cancelled' AND s.end_day>=294 AND s.end_day<301 AND c.customer_type='small'
GROUP BY c.group_id, s.plan, age_band ORDER BY cancels DESC
"""), 100)
print_rows('new subs days 294-300 by group plan', q("""
SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(*) AS n, ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) AS avg_eff, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) AS mrr, MIN(s.start_day), MAX(s.start_day)
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND s.start_day>=294 AND s.start_day<301 AND c.customer_type='small'
GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan ORDER BY n DESC
"""), 100)
# issues active only open
print_rows('active open issues by group age', q("""
SELECT i.group_id, i.days_open, COUNT(*) AS n
FROM issues i JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=i.customer_id AND s.status='subscribed'
WHERE i.status='open'
GROUP BY i.group_id, i.days_open ORDER BY i.group_id, i.days_open
"""), 100)
print_rows('issues opened/resolved last7 active groups', q("""
SELECT group_id,
 SUM(CASE WHEN open_day>=294 AND open_day<301 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS opened,
 SUM(CASE WHEN resolved_day>=294 AND resolved_day<301 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS resolved,
 SUM(CASE WHEN status='open' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS open_total,
 AVG(CASE WHEN status='open' THEN days_open END) AS avg_open_age,
 MAX(CASE WHEN status='open' THEN days_open END) AS max_open_age
FROM issues
GROUP BY group_id ORDER BY open_total DESC
"""), 50)
# usage by group/plan last 7
print_rows('usage last7 by group plan active-ish', q("""
SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(DISTINCT du.customer_id) AS users,
 ROUND(SUM(du.usage_units),0) AS usage,
 ROUND(AVG(du.usage_units),1) AS avg_daily_user,
 ROUND(SUM(du.usage_units)*CASE s.plan WHEN 'A' THEN 0.002 WHEN 'B' THEN 0.006 WHEN 'C' THEN 0.03 ELSE 0 END,2) AS est_compute
FROM daily_usage du JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=du.customer_id AND s.status='subscribed'
WHERE du.day>=294 AND du.day<301 AND c.customer_type='small'
GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan ORDER BY usage DESC
"""), 100)
print_rows('usage by day', q("""
SELECT day, ROUND(SUM(usage_units),0) AS usage, COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id) AS users
FROM daily_usage WHERE day>=294 GROUP BY day ORDER BY day
"""), 20)
# enterprise threads overview
print_rows('enterprise active subscribed seats', q("""
SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(*) AS customers, SUM(s.seat_count) AS seats, ROUND(SUM(s.seat_count*s.effective_price),2) AS mrr
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='large'
GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan
"""), 50)
print_rows('enterprise threads open by latest sender/day/type', q("""
WITH latest AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) AS mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id),
     l AS (SELECT e.* FROM enterprise_turns e JOIN latest ON e.message_id=latest.mid)
SELECT day, sender, thread_type, COUNT(*) AS threads, SUM(seat_count) AS latest_seats
FROM l GROUP BY day,sender,thread_type ORDER BY day DESC,sender,thread_type
"""), 100)
print_rows('enterprise open latest group/day/sender', q("""
WITH latest AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) AS mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id),
     l AS (SELECT e.* FROM enterprise_turns e JOIN latest ON e.message_id=latest.mid),
     sys AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(seat_count) AS sys_seats FROM enterprise_turns WHERE sender='system' GROUP BY customer_id)
SELECT c.group_id, l.day, l.sender, l.thread_type, COUNT(*) AS threads, SUM(sys.sys_seats) AS seats
FROM l JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) LEFT JOIN sys USING(customer_id)
GROUP BY c.group_id,l.day,l.sender,l.thread_type ORDER BY l.day DESC, seats DESC
"""), 200)
print_rows('enterprise closed last14', q("""
SELECT day, close_reason, thread_type, sender, COUNT(*) AS rows, SUM(seat_count) AS seats
FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=1 AND day>=287 GROUP BY day,close_reason,thread_type,sender ORDER BY day DESC
"""), 100)
# sample enterprise latest customer messages
rows=q("""
WITH latest AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) AS mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id)
SELECT e.message_id,e.customer_id,e.day,e.thread_type,e.turn_number,e.sender,e.seat_count,e.message_text,e.offer_json,c.group_id,c.company_size_descriptor,c.company_primary_concern,c.company_decision_style
FROM enterprise_turns e JOIN latest ON e.message_id=latest.mid JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE e.sender!='agent'
ORDER BY e.day DESC, e.message_id DESC LIMIT 20
""")
print_rows('sample non-agent latest enterprise threads', rows, 20)
